description: Use this API to add or modify all OPF adjustable bus shunt records in a specified subsystem of the working case. This API routine was first introduced in release 28.0.0. ierr = opf_adjvar_subsys(sid, all, autoadd, shntid, intgar, realar, useval)
python_syntax: ierr = opf_adjvar_subsys(sid, all, autoadd, shntid, intgar, realar, useval)

parameters.name: sid
parameters.description: A valid subsystem id from 0 to 11. a subsystem must have been previously defined. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: all
parameters.description: All buses or specified subsystem flag. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: autoadd
parameters.description: Indicates whether new records can be added (default treatment same as value of 3). (input).

parameters.name: shntid
parameters.description: Adjustable bus shunt identifier. (input; ‘1’ by default).

parameters.name: useval
parameters.description: An array of four elements specifying Used to indicate actions to be taken on the REALAR values. If USEVAL is 1 then value REALAR value is multiplied by the current value. If USEVAL is 2 then REALAR value is added to current real value. If USEVAL is 0 (default) then REALAR is used as provided. (input).

autoadd.description: do not add a new var record if it does not already exist.
add a new var record if it does not already exist.
add a separate shunt record at the bus.
only modify existing bus shunt records that correspond to the shunt identifier given in SHNTID.

intgar.description: cost curve type. This value is not currently used by the program.
status (default value of 1 for new records, current value otherwise).
cost table cross reference number. This value is not currently used by the program.
indicates which bus types should be processed (default treatment same as value of 0).
process only Type 1 buses in subsystem.
process only Type 2 and 3 buses in subsystem.

realar.description: bus shunt susceptance in Mvar at nominal voltage (default value of 0.0 for new records, current values used for existing ones).
maximum bus shunt susceptance in Mvar (default value of 0.0 for new records, current value otherwise).
minimum bus shunt susceptance in Mvar (default value of 0.0 for new records, current value otherwise).
cost scale coefficient in $/Mvar (default value of 1.0 for new records, current value otherwise).

useval.description: indicates how the value of REALAR(1) should be adjusted.
if the value of REALAR(1) is to be used as defined.
if the value of REALAR(1) is to be multiplied by the present value of the bus shunt susceptance.
if the value of REALAR(1) is to be added to the present value of the bus shunt susceptance.
indicates how the value of REALAR(2) should be used.
if the value of REALAR(2) is to be used as defined.
if the value of REALAR(2) is to be multiplied by the present value of the maximum bus shunt susceptance.
if the value of REALAR(2) is to be added to the present value of the maximum bus shunt susceptance.
indicates how the value of REALAR(3) should be used.
if the value of REALAR(3) is to be used as defined.
if the value of REALAR(3) is to be multiplied by the present value of the minimum bus shunt susceptance.
if the value of REALAR(3) is to be added to the present value of the minimum bus shunt susceptance.
indicates how the value of REALAR(4) should be used.
if the value of REALAR(4) is to be used as defined.
if the value of REALAR(4) is to be multiplied by the present value of the cost scale coefficient.
if the value of REALAR(4) is to be added to the present value of the cost scale coefficient.

error_codes.description: adjustable bus shunt identifier is more than two characters.
invalid SID value or subsystem SID is not defined.
adjustable bus shunt table is full.
node-breaker terminal connection table is full.
prerequisite requirements for API are not met.
warnings - one or more of: - Invalid cost curve type specified. - Invalid adjustable bus shunt status specified. - Maximum shunt susceptance less than minimum shunt susceptance.
